chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. S – 88 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.243/2025 (Ex./A) 2. For hearing of main case 16.06.2025 None present, nor has any intimation been received despite issuance of intimation notice to the Counsel for the petitioner. Record reflects that prior to approaching this Court, the petitioner had filed an application under Sections 22-A & B, Cr.P.C., bearing Cr. M.A No.748/2025 before the learned Additional Sessions Judge / Justice of Peace, Gambat, which was allowed vide order dated 06.03.2025, wherein protection had already been granted. The operative para of the said order is reproduced below: “3. Heard & perused. Applicant filed petition under section 22-A & 22-B Cr.P.C seeking directions to the respondents not to harass the applicant & her family members on behest of private respondents. The official respondents have denied allegations of harassment and submitted that they will not harass the applicant. Every citizen is entitled to protection of law and no one, including the private respondents, has authority to disturb the life & liberty of the citizen, which is protected under Articles 4 & 9 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, which postulates that it is inalienable right of every citizen to be treated in accordance with law and no action detrimental to the reputation, life and liberty, shall be taken, except, as per law. Therefore, application in hand is allowed with directions to the respondents to provide appropriate legal protection, albeit; any application by or against applicant, shall be dealt in accordance with law.” Perusal of the prayer clause of the instant petition reflects that it is identical in nature and a repetition of the same relief sought earlier.

Every citizen is entitled to protection of law and no one, including the private respondents, has authority to disturb the life & liberty of the citizen, which is protected under Articles 4 & 9 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, which postulates that it is inalienable right of every citizen to be treated in accordance with law and no action detrimental to the reputation, life and liberty, shall be taken, except, as per law. Therefore, application in hand is allowed with directions to the respondents to provide appropriate legal protection, albeit; any application by or against applicant, shall be dealt in accordance with law.” Perusal of the prayer clause of the instant petition reflects that it is identical in nature and a repetition of the same relief sought earlier. Accordingly, the petition stands dismissed for non-prosecution along with the listed application. J U D G E Abdul Basit
